content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660                    322 5901            322 6441           322 6444

July 12, 1978

Mr. Noel Robinson
Franchise Tax Board
Post Office Box 1468
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Robinson:

This letter will confirm the advice I gave to you on June 29, 1978.

First, you asked whether an attorney would have to disqualify himself from participating in an audit in the following hypothetical situation. The attorney owns $1,000 of stock in X corporation. Together, corporations X and Y own Z, a subsidiary corporation. You asked under what circumstances would the attorney be disqualified from participating in an audit of any of these entities.

Since the attorney owns a thousand dollars of stock in X corporation, he has an investment in X corporation within the meaning of Section 87103. Therefore, he would be disqualified from participating in an audit of X corporation if it is foreseeable that the audit would have a material effect on X. Assuming that the attorney owns less than 50% of the stock in X corporation, he would be disqualified from participating in the audit of Y or Z only if it were foreseeable that the audit would materially affect X corporation. In the event the attorney had owned 50% or more of X corporation, he would be disqualified from participating in the audit of Z corporation if it is foreseeable that the audit would have a material financial effect on Z. This result is dictated by the last paragraph of Section 87103.1/

1/ The last paragraph of Section 87103 provides as follows:

For purposes of this section, indirect investment or interest means any investment or interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of a public official, by an agent on behalf of a public official or by a trust in which he has a substantial interest. A business entity is controlled by a public official if the public official, his agents, spouse and dependent children hold more than fifty percent of the ownership interest in the entity. A public official has a substantial interest in a trust when the official, his spouse and dependent children have a present or future interest worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000).

Mr. Noel Robinson
July 12, 1978
Page Two

Therefore, when the official owns 50% or more of X, he will be disqualified
from participation in decisions which materially affect Z, even in those
cases where the decision would not have a material financial effect on X.

I also explained how this interpretation was consistent with the Biondo
opinion, 1 FPPC Ops. 54 (July 2, 1975, No. 75-036). You will recall that
opinion involved a public official who owned stock in San Diego Gas
and Electric Company. San Diego Gas and Electric, along with another corporation, was involved in a joint venture. The question was whether the public
official could participate in a decision affecting the joint venture. The
Biondo opinion concluded that the public official was disqualified from
participating in the decision in question because the effects of the joint
venture would be so significant as to materially affect San Diego Gas and
Electric. Thus, the Biondo opinion did not require disqualification merely
because San Diego Gas and Electric was a participant in the joint venture;
rather, disqualification was required because San Diego Gas and Electric would
be materially affected by decisions affecting the joint venture.

Second, you asked whether an attorney who owns shares in a mutual fund
could participate in an audit of an investment held by the mutual fund.
Again, the attorney would be disqualified if the audit would materially
affect the mutual fund. However, if the audit would not materially affect
the mutual fund itself, the attorney would be disqualified only if he owned
50% or more of the mutual fund and the audit would materially affect the
investment held by the mutual fund.

If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact
me.

Sincerely,

Ted Prim
Chief, Conflicts of
Interest Division

TP:mfa
